channel.title: Posts on Fossa Method
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Fossa Method

item.title: Cryptocaryon irritans: Not Just White Spots
item.description: Cryptocaryon irritans. is a ciliated protozoan parasite, often visible to the naked eye. Tangs, butterflyfish, and boxfish are particularly susceptible and typically among the first to become infected. The free-swimming infectious stage — theront — most often attaches to gills and fins. That’s why these areas should always be examined first
